rules: Count the number of cards in your discard pile and shuffle them into your deck. Then discard that many cards from the top of your deck.
Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws a new hand of the same number of cards.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

No player may have more than 4 Pokémon on his or her Bench. When this card is played, if a player has 5 Pokémon on his or her Bench, that player chooses 1 of them and returns it and all cards attached to it to his or her hand. (If both players have to return a Pokémon, your opponent returns a Pokémon first.)

You and your opponent play a game of Rock-Paper-Scissors. The winner shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws a new hand of 5 cards. (If you don't know how to play Rock-Paper-Scissors, flip a coin to decide who's the winner.)
For each of your Pokémon, you may return any number of Energy cards attached to it to your hand.
Discard any number of other cards from your hand, then flip a coin. If heads, draw twice that many cards.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Whenever a player attacks with a Pokémon with Lt. Surge in its name, he or she may flip a coin. If heads, and if that Pokémon's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon. If tails, the attacking Pokémon does 10 damage to itself in addition to whatever its attack usually does.

Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent sets aside all the cards in his or her hand face down. Nobody may look at those cards. At the end of your opponent's next turn, your opponent puts those cards back into his or her hand. If tails, your turn ends immediately (you can't attack this turn).
Look at your opponent's hand. Then, you may discard as many other cards as you want from your hand and draw that many cards.

Attach Sabrina's ESP to 1 of your Pokémon with Sabrina in its name. At the end of your turn, discard Sabrina's ESP. If that Pokémon uses and attack that involves flipping coins, Sabrina's ESP lets you re-flip those coins once. If you do, re-flip all the coins.
For your attack this turn, your Active Pokémon can use any attack from its Basic Pokémon card or any Evolution card attached to it. (You still have to pay for that attack's Energy cost.)

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Don't apply Resistance to any attacks made by Pokémon with Brock in their names.
Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. Choose 2 of those cards and put them into your hand. Discard the rest.

fullDisplayName: Minion of Team Rocket 113/132 Gym Heroes
rules: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and return it and all cards attached to it to his or her hand. If 1 or both of them are tails, your turn ends immediately (you can't attack this turn).
Your opponent chooses 1 of the following: everyone chooses 1 of his or her own Prizes and put it into his or her hand, or you draw a card.

In order to play this card, you can't have any Basic Pokémon cards in your hand. Show your hand to your opponent, then search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Basic Pokémon cards there, you may put any number of them onto your opponent's Bench (as long as there's room).

Trade 2 of the other cards in your hand for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and/or Evolution cards with Erika in their names from your deck. Show those cards to your opponent, then put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Each player pays Colorless less to retreat his or her Pokémon if it has Misty in its name.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

During each player's turn, that player may choose to discard an Energy card attached to 1 of his or her Pokémon with Erika in its name. If that player does so, that Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.
Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card with Brock in its name. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You can't play this card if you have any cards in your hand other than Blaine's Last Resort. Show your hand to your opponent, then draw 5 cards.

fullDisplayName: The Rocket's Training Gym 104/132 Gym Heroes
rules:
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Each player pays Colorless more to retreat his or her Active Pokémon.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

A player must discard 2 cards from his or her hand in order to play an Energy Removal or Super Energy Removal card.

Discard 2 of the other cards in your hand in order to play this card. If this turn's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and if the attacking Pokémon has Misty in its name, the attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

You can play this card only if you have at least 1 Basic Pokémon card in your hand. Put a Basic Pokémon card from your hand into play as your Active Pokémon. Put your old Active Pokémon onto your Bench. (You can't play this card if your Bench is full.)
You may draw up to 3 cards, then your opponent may draw up to 3 cards.

Attach Charity to your Active Pokémon. Unless that Pokémon gets Knocked Out, return Charity to your hand at the end of your turn. If that Pokémon attacks and does damage to the Defending Pokémon, you may reduce that damage by any amount (rounded to the nearest 10).
Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

Put a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from your hand face down in front of you and tell your opponent its name. Your opponent guesses the length of that Pokémon. Flip the card over. If your opponent guessed right, he or she draws 2 cards. If your opponent guessed wrong, you draw 2 cards. Either way, return the card to your hand.

attacks.name: Poison Antennae
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Removal Beam
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and discard it.

attacks.name: Lazy Attack
attacks.effect: Sabrina's Slowpoke is now Asleep (after doing damage).

attacks.name: Sleight of Hand
attacks.effect: Choose up to 3 cards from your hand and put them on top of your deck. Then search your deck for that many basic Energy cards. Show those cards to your opponent, then put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Spook

attacks.name: Suggestion
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Energy Loop

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Pokémon Powers, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Clamp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Spin Ball
attacks.effect: Flip 1 coin. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Razor Wind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.effect: During your next turn, Lt. Surge's Rattata's Gnaw attack's base damage is doubled.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.effect: Take 1 Lightning Energy card from your discard pile and attach it to Lt. Surge's Pikachu.

attacks.name: Lightning Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Stretch Vine
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.effect: If there are any damage counters on Erika's Oddish, remove 1 of them.

attacks.name: Sporadic Sponging
attacks.effect: If Erika's Oddish has any damage counters on it, flip a coin. If heads, remove 1 of those damage counters.

attacks.name: Eggsplosion
attacks.effect: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy attached to Erika's Exeggcute. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Careless Tackle
attacks.effect: Erika's Bellsprout does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Poison Vine
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Poison Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Sand Pit
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Brock's Sandshrew during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If 1 or both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Bellow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.effect: Shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.effect: Does 40 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Brock's Mankey.

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Call for Friend
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card with Brock in its name and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

attacks.effect: Don't apply Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Resistance still happen.)

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads to the Defending Pokémon and 20 damage times the number of tails to Blaine's Tauros.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Blaine's Ponyta.

attacks.name: Blaze
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to each Grass Pokémon on your opponent's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Kindle
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Blaine's Charmander in order to use this attack. If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

attacks.name: Naptime
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 3 damage counters from Sabrina's Slowbro and Sabrina's Slowbro is now Asleep. If Sabrina's Slowbro has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

attacks.name: Screaming Headbutt
attacks.effect: You can't use this attack during your next turn.

attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't attach Energy cards to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Misty's Starmie but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mud Splash
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Call for Friend
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for a Basic Pokémon with Misty in its name and put it onto your Bench. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and discard it.

attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and discard it.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.effect: Both the Defending Pokémon and Erika's Gloom are now Asleep (after doing damage).

attacks.name: Vile Smell
attacks.effect: Both the Defending Pokémon and Erika's Gloom are now Confused (after doing damage).

attacks.name: Healing Pollen
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 4 damage counters from Erika's Gloom. If Erika's Gloom has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

attacks.name: Magic Pollen
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned (your choice).

attacks.name: Psychic Exchange
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 5 cards.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage.

attacks.name: Deflector
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, whenever Erika's Exeggcute takes damage, divide that damage in half (rounded down to the nearest 10). (Any other effects still happen.)

attacks.name: Egg Bomb
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing to the Defending Pokémon and Erika's Exeggcute does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Tail Strike
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rock Toss
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

attacks.name: Lucky Shot
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Lava Burst
attacks.effect: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. (If there are fewer than 5 cards in your deck, discard all of them.) This attack does 20 damage for each Fire Energy card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Child's Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: One-Two Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage.

attacks.name: Shake
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Fire Tackle
attacks.effect: Blaine's Growlithe does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Healing Pollen
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. If a Pokémon has fewer damage counters than the number of heads, remove all of them.

attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If 1 or both of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Collapse

attacks.name: Water Ring
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to each Pokémon that isn't Water on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Horn Hazard
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Triple Cannon
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.effect: Discard all Energy cards attached to Lt. Surge's Raichu in order to use this attack.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.effect: Take up to 2 Lightning Energy cards from your discard pile and attach them to Lt. Surge's Electabuzz.

attacks.name: Electric Current
attacks.effect: Take 1 Lightning Energy card attached to Lt. Surge's Electabuzz and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you have no Benched Pokémon, discard that Energy card.

attacks.name: Moonwatching
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a basic Energy card. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Comet Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Alert
attacks.effect: Draw a card. Then, switch Brock's Zubat with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. You can't use this attack if your Bench is empty.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Horn Toss
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: Brock's Rhyhorn does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Tunneling

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose up to 3 of them. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Flip a coin. If heads, choose up to 3 cards at random from your opponent's hand (don't look at them). Your opponent shuffles those cards into his or her deck.

Discard 2 of the other cards in your hand in order to play this card. If this turn's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and if the attacking Pokémon has Misty in its name, the attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

You can play this card only if you have at least 1 Basic Pokémon card in your hand. Put a Basic Pokémon card from your hand into play as your Active Pokémon. Put your old Active Pokémon onto your Bench. (You can't play this card if your Bench is full.)
You may draw up to 3 cards, then your opponent may draw up to 3 cards.
Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Pain Amplifier
attacks.effect: Put a damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon has already has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Call of the Night
attacks.effect: Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent shuffles his or her Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Shadow Images
attacks.effect: Whenever Rocket's Scyther is attacked, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does no damage to Rocket's Scyther. (Any other effects of the attack still happen.) This effect lasts until Rocket's Scyther takes damage (or is Benched or is evolved).

attacks.name: Fire Wall
attacks.effect: If an attack does damage to Rocket's Moltres during your opponent's next turn (even if Rocket's Moltres is Knocked Out), Rocket's Moltres attacks your opponent's Active Pokémon for 10 damage. (Apply Weakness and Resistance.)

attacks.name: Crosscounter
attacks.effect: If an attack does damage to Rocket's Hitmonchan during your opponent's next turn (even if Rocket's Hitmonchan is Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, Rocket's Hitmonchan attacks your opponent's Active Pokémon for double that amount of damage. (If Rocket's Hitmonchan takes 20 damage, it does 40 damage to that Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Jellyfish Poison
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Knockout Needle
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 60 more damage. If 1 or both of them are tails, this attack does 30 damage.

attacks.name: Mega Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Lt. Surge's Magneton does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Repeating Drill
attacks.effect: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.effect: Take up to 2 Lightning Energy cards from your discard pile and attach them to Lt. Surge's Electabuzz.

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.effect: Discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Lt. Surge's Electabuzz in order to use this attack. Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Lightning Energy cards you discarded. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: If Erika's Vileplume does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove a number of damage counters from Erika's Vileplume equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Erika's Vileplume has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Take Away
attacks.effect: Shuffle Erika's Dragonair and all cards attached to it into your deck. Then, your opponent shuffles his or her Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Fairy Power
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, you may return any number of your Pokémon in play and all cards attached to them to your hand.

attacks.name: Lariat
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Phoenix Flame
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, shuffle Blaine's Moltres and all cards attached to it into your deck (after doing damage).
